title: Marketing Analytics
text: Below are selected projects that highlight my experience applying marketing analytics and strategic research to real-world challenges. These projects reflect my ability to combine data analysis, business research, and strategic thinking to inform decisions and drive results.

title: Education
JPMorgan Chase Interim Management Report
text:
Overview
This group management report provided a comprehensive analysis of JPMorgan Chase, covering its general and specific environmental factors, internal operations, and strategic positioning. Sections included technological advances, industry regulations, competitor analysis, leadership, and a full SWOT analysis with actionable recommendations.




Learning Outcomes

Conduct a full internal and external environmental analysis of a major corporation.
Evaluate competitive positioning using frameworks like SWOT.
Assess leadership style and its impact on organizational culture.
Identify strategic opportunities and threats in the financial services industry.
Develop practical, evidence-based business recommendations.




Key Skills Gained

Environmental and competitor analysis
Strategic management frameworks
Business research and source evaluation
SWOT analysis
Collaborative report writing

title: Research Participation Data Analysis
text:
Overview
This collaborative group project analyzed research participation data using R and SPSS. The team cleaned and wrangled the dataset, built visualizations with ggplot2, and published the final report as a Quarto document to GitHub Pages. The project was completed as part of a marketing analytics course at Cal Poly Pomona.




Learning Outcomes

Clean and prepare real-world survey data for analysis.
Build and interpret visualizations using ggplot2.
Collaborate on a data project with defined team roles.
Publish a reproducible report using Quarto and GitHub Pages.
Draw marketing insights from participation pattern data.




Key Skills Gained

R and ggplot2
Data wrangling
Quarto report publishing
GitHub Pages deployment
Team-based data analysis

objectID: listings/01-Student Financial Stress & Mental Health Analysis.html
href: listings/01-Student Financial Stress & Mental Health Analysis.html
title: Student Financial Stress & Mental Health Analysis
text:
Overview
This group research project investigated the relationship between financial insecurity and mental health among Cal Poly Pomona students. Using a quantitative survey from a BUS1010 class, the team analyzed responses to identify patterns in stress, depression, and financial burden, then proposed campus-level recommendations aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goal of ending poverty.




Learning Outcomes

Analyze survey data to identify trends in student wellbeing.
Connect financial insecurity to measurable mental health outcomes.
Develop evidence-based recommendations for institutional change.
Communicate research findings in a structured academic format.
Align research with global sustainability frameworks.




Key Skills Gained

Survey design and data collection
Quantitative data analysis
Academic research and citation
Team collaboration
Policy and recommendation writing

title: Strategic Marketing
text: Below are selected projects that showcase my work in strategic marketing, campaign development, and brand management. These examples highlight how I apply leadership, data-informed decision making, and creative direction to drive marketing success.

title: Strategic Marketing
section: Socio-Economic Data Analysis & Stakeholder Advocacy — BUS 1010
text:
Socio-Economic Data Analysis & Stakeholder Advocacy — BUS 1010
In this project, I led a cross-functional research initiative to quantify the correlation between financial insecurity and academic performance at Cal Poly Pomona. From survey design to stakeholder presentation, I managed the full lifecycle of the campaign, aligning findings with institutional objectives and actionable recommendations.
Skills Demonstrated:

Marketing Strategy: Designed a survey framework capturing data from 40 students to surface key service gaps in campus resources.
Team Coordination: Collaborated across a research team to aggregate and interpret data using Excel and Google Forms.
Performance Tracking: Translated raw data into measurable insights identifying critical gaps in mental health and financial support.
Brand Positioning: Delivered a persuasive narrative to the University President and Dean that influenced resource reallocation decisions.

title: Strategic Marketing
section: Corporate Brand Analysis — Principles of Management — IBM 3102
text:
Corporate Brand Analysis — Principles of Management — IBM 3102
This initiative involved conducting a full corporate analysis including SWOT, VRIO, and internal/external environment assessments for a real organization. I synthesized competitive insights into a strategic report and presentation that earned a 95% and 100% respectively.
Skills Demonstrated:

Brand Analysis: Audited organizational strengths, weaknesses, and market positioning through SWOT and VRIO frameworks.
Competitive Benchmarking: Evaluated the company’s standing within its competitive landscape using professional research databases.
Strategic Communication: Developed clear value propositions and a cohesive narrative for a general business audience.
Stakeholder Presentation: Delivered findings via a Prezi presentation and Camtasia-produced video to course instructors.

Below is an embedded demonstration of the interactive RevealJS:

title: Strategic Marketing
section: Student Outcomes & Equity Data Analysis — IBM 3202
text:
Student Outcomes & Equity Data Analysis — IBM 3202
For this research project, I collaborated with a team to analyze a CPP student dataset in RStudio, investigating how race, gender, and major relate to learning abilities and disabilities. I developed the analytical approach and led the class presentation of findings.
Skills Demonstrated:

Go-to-Market Strategy: Structured the research process from data cleaning to insight delivery with a clear audience in mind.
Integrated Marketing: Combined statistical analysis (ANOVA, Cronbach’s Alpha) with accessible storytelling for a non-technical audience.
Budget Management: Worked within academic constraints to deliver a polished, professional output using RStudio and descriptive statistics.
Launch Optimization: Refined our analytical approach based on p-value significance thresholds and peer feedback during the presentation.

Below is an embedded demonstration of the interactive RevealJS:

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
Service Operations & Guest Experience — Chick-fil-A
As a Shift Lead managing 200+ daily guests and a 15+ member team, I continuously assessed and optimized front-of-house operations to improve guest satisfaction and team performance — applying the same principles as a marketing consultant auditing a brand’s customer experience.
Skills Demonstrated:

Client Consulting: Identified operational pain points and delivered real-time adjustments to improve guest experience and service flow.
Persona Development: Observed guest behavior patterns during national promotions and mobile app campaigns to anticipate needs and adjust workflows.
Campaign Optimization: Monitored service metrics including order accuracy and speed to identify underperformance and drive improvements.
Data-Driven Adjustments: Used service data and shift observations to inform staffing decisions and maintain brand standards.
